steps.title: Spot the journalist's core claim
steps.content: The journalist argues that Oscar nominations drive long-term success even when opening weekends are modest — the example must show this pattern.

steps.title: Match the pattern to the choices
steps.content: Amélie (Oscar-nominated, opening weekend \$136,470, lifetime \$33,225,499) vastly outearned The Girl Who Played with Fire (not nominated, opening weekend \$904,998, lifetime \$7,638,241), showing how Oscar recognition boosted long-term success despite a much weaker opening. The other choices either lack an Oscar/non-Oscar comparison (A), compare only two Oscar-nominated films (C), or show a non-nominated film earning more than a nominated one (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
